sentences.sentence: Everyone knows the situation where you can't get a song out of your head.
sentences.translation: 每个人都经历过一首歌在脑中挥之不去的情况。

structure.text: where you can't get a song out of your head

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰抽象名词 situation。
get a song out of your head 表示让一首歌不再萦绕脑海。

expressions.text: get something out of your head

sentences.sentence: You hear a pop song on the radio - or even just read the song's title and it haunts you for hours, playing over and over in your mind until you're heartily sick of it.
sentences.translation: 你在收音机里听到一首流行歌，甚至只是读到歌名，它便会在你脑中反复播放、纠缠数小时，直到你彻底厌烦。

structure.text: You hear a pop song on the radio
or even just read the song's title
and it haunts you for hours
playing over and over in your mind
until you're heartily sick of it

difficult_points: read 与 hear 共享主语 You，破折号后的 or 引出更极端的情况。
playing... 的逻辑主语是 it，即那首歌；until 从句表示持续到何时。

sentences.sentence: The condition now even has a medical name 'song-in-head syndrome'.
sentences.translation: 这种状况如今甚至有了一个医学名称：“脑中歌曲综合征”。
difficult_points: The condition 回指歌曲在脑中挥之不去的状况。
song-in-head syndrome 是 a medical name 的同位说明。

sentences.sentence: But why does the mind annoy us like this?
sentences.translation: 但大脑为什么会这样折磨我们呢？
difficult_points: does 提前构成特殊疑问句，实义动词 annoy 使用原形。
like this 回指歌曲在脑中循环播放的现象。

sentences.sentence: No one knows for sure, but it's probably because the brain is better at holding onto information than it is at knowing what information is important.
sentences.translation: 没人确切知道原因，但这可能是因为大脑更擅长留存信息，却不那么善于判断哪些信息重要。

structure.text: the brain is better at holding onto information
than it is at knowing what information is important

difficult_points: because 从句解释可能的原因。
better at A than at B 比较大脑的两种能力；what 引导 knowing 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Roger Chaffin, a psychologist at the University of Connecticut says, 'It's a manifestation of an aspect of memory which is normally an asset to us, but in this instance it can be a nuisance.'
sentences.translation: 康涅狄格大学心理学家罗杰·查芬说：“这是记忆某一特性的表现；这种特性通常对我们有益，但在这种情况下却可能令人困扰。”

structure.text: a psychologist at the University of Connecticut
It's a manifestation of an aspect of memory
which is normally an asset to us
but in this instance it can be a nuisance

difficult_points: a psychologist... 是 Roger Chaffin 的同位语。
which 修饰 an aspect of memory；后一个 it 也回指这一记忆特性。

sentences.sentence: This eager acquisitiveness of the brain may have helped our ancestors remember important information in the past.
sentences.translation: 大脑这种积极获取信息的特性，过去可能帮助我们的祖先记住重要信息。

structure.text: This eager acquisitiveness of the brain
remember important information in the past

difficult_points: may have helped 表示对过去情况的推测。
help 后接宾语 our ancestors 和省略 to 的不定式 remember。

sentences.sentence: Today, students use it to learn new material, and musicians rely on it to memorise complicated pieces.
sentences.translation: 如今，学生利用它学习新内容，音乐家则依靠它记忆复杂的乐曲。

structure.text: students use it to learn new material
and musicians rely on it to memorise complicated pieces

difficult_points: 两个 it 都指大脑主动留存信息的能力。
to learn 与 to memorise 都是目的不定式。

sentences.sentence: But when this useful function goes awry it can get you stuck on a tune.
sentences.translation: 但当这种有用的功能出现偏差时，它会让一段旋律困在你的脑海里。

structure.text: when this useful function goes awry

difficult_points: when 从句后原文未加逗号，但不影响其状语功能。
get＋宾语＋过去分词补语表示使某人陷入某种状态。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, superficial, repetitive pop tunes are, by their very nature, more likely to stick than something more inventive.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，肤浅、重复的流行旋律从本质上就比更有创意的音乐更容易挥之不去。
difficult_points: by their very nature 是插入语，表示这种倾向源于流行旋律本身的特性。
more likely... than... 构成比较，something more inventive 指更有创造性的音乐。

sentences.sentence: The annoying playback probably originates in the auditory cortex.
sentences.translation: 这种烦人的重复播放可能源于听觉皮层。
difficult_points: playback 在此指歌曲在脑内的重复回放。
probably 表明这是推测而非完全确定的结论。

sentences.sentence: Located at the front of the brain, this region handles both listening and playback of music and other sounds.
sentences.translation: 这一区域位于大脑前部，负责音乐及其他声音的聆听与回放。

structure.text: Located at the front of the brain
both listening and playback of music and other sounds

difficult_points: Located... 是过去分词状语，其逻辑主语是 this region。
both A and B 连接 listening 与 playback 两项功能。

sentences.sentence: Neuroscientist Robert Zatorre of McGill University in Montreal proved this some years ago when he asked volunteers to replay the theme from the TV show Dallas in their heads.
sentences.translation: 几年前，蒙特利尔麦吉尔大学的神经科学家罗伯特·扎托雷让志愿者在脑中重放电视剧《达拉斯》的主题曲，由此证明了这一点。

structure.text: Neuroscientist Robert Zatorre of McGill University in Montreal
to replay the theme from the TV show Dallas in their heads

difficult_points: this 回指脑内回放源于听觉皮层的观点。
ask volunteers to replay 是 ask＋宾语＋不定式补语结构。

sentences.sentence: Brain imaging studies showed that this activated the same region of the auditory cortex as when the people actually heard the song.
sentences.translation: 脑成像研究显示，这种脑内重放激活的听觉皮层区域，与人们实际听到歌曲时被激活的区域相同。

structure.text: that this activated the same region of the auditory cortex
as when the people actually heard the song

difficult_points: this 指志愿者在脑中重放主题曲这一行为。
the same... as... 构成比较；when 从句代表实际听歌时的情形。

sentences.sentence: Not every stored musical memory emerges into consciousness, however.
sentences.translation: 不过，并非每一段储存的音乐记忆都会进入意识。
difficult_points: Not every 是部分否定，意为“并非每一个”，不是“没有任何一个”。
however 后置，提示与前文所述脑内回放现象形成限定。

sentences.sentence: The frontal lobe of the brain gets to decide which thoughts become conscious and which ones are simply stored away.
sentences.translation: 大脑额叶负责决定哪些想法进入意识，哪些只被储存起来。

structure.text: The frontal lobe of the brain
and which ones are simply stored away

difficult_points: decide 后接两个由 and 连接的 which 宾语从句。
ones 替代 thoughts；are stored away 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: But it can become fatigued or depressed, which is when people most commonly suffer from song-in-head syndrome and other intrusive thoughts, says Susan Ball, a clinical psychologist at Indiana University School of Medicine in Indianapolis.
sentences.translation: 但额叶可能会疲劳或功能低下，而这正是人们最常出现“脑中歌曲综合征”和其他侵入性思维的时候，印第安纳大学医学院临床心理学家苏珊·鲍尔说道。

structure.text: But it can become fatigued or depressed
which is when people most commonly suffer from song-in-head syndrome and other intrusive thoughts
a clinical psychologist at Indiana University School of Medicine in Indianapolis

difficult_points: it 指上一句的 frontal lobe。
which 指代额叶疲劳或低迷这一整体情况，并通过 when 说明此时容易出现症状。

sentences.sentence: And once the unwanted song surfaces, it's hard to stuff it back down into the subconscious.
sentences.translation: 而且，一旦那首不想听的歌浮现出来，就很难再把它塞回潜意识。

structure.text: to stuff it back down into the subconscious

difficult_points: once 引导时间兼条件状语从句，表示歌曲一旦进入意识。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是后面的不定式；不定式中的 it 指 the unwanted song。

sentences.sentence: 'The more you try to suppress a thought, the more you get it,' says Ball.
sentences.translation: 鲍尔说：“你越想压制一个念头，它反而越会出现。”

structure.text: The more you try to suppress a thought

difficult_points: the more..., the more... 表示“越……越……”。
it 指前面的 a thought；get 在此表示这个念头不断出现。

sentences.sentence: 'We call this the pink elephant phenomenon.
sentences.translation: “我们把这种现象称为‘粉红大象现象’。
difficult_points: call A B 是“把 A 称为 B”的宾语补足语结构。
原文在本句末没有闭合引号，引语延续至下一句。

sentences.sentence: Tell the brain not to think about pink elephants, and it's guaranteed to do so,' she says.
sentences.translation: 告诉大脑不要去想粉红大象，它却一定会去想。”她说道。

structure.text: Tell the brain not to think about pink elephants
and it's guaranteed to do so

difficult_points: 祈使句＋and＋陈述句在此表示一种必然结果。
it 指 the brain，do so 替代 think about pink elephants。

sentences.sentence: For those not severely afflicted, simply avoiding certain kinds of music can help.
sentences.translation: 对于症状不严重的人来说，只要避开某些类型的音乐就会有所帮助。

structure.text: simply avoiding certain kinds of music

difficult_points: not severely afflicted 是过去分词短语，修饰 those。
avoiding... 是动名词短语，充当句子主语。

sentences.sentence: 'I know certain pieces that are kind of "sticky" to me, so I will not play them in the early morning for fear that they will run around in my head all day,' says Steven Brown, who trained as a classical pianist but is now a neuroscientist at the University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio.
sentences.translation: 史蒂文·布朗说：“我知道有些曲子对我来说特别容易黏在脑中，所以我不会一大早播放它们，免得它们整天在我脑海里打转。”他曾受过古典钢琴训练，如今是得克萨斯大学圣安东尼奥健康科学中心的神经科学家。

structure.text: that are kind of "sticky" to me
so I will not play them in the early morning
for fear that they will run around in my head all day
who trained as a classical pianist but is now a neuroscientist at the University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio

difficult_points: 第一个 that 修饰 pieces；them 与后一个 they 都指这些曲子。
for fear that 表示“唯恐……”，说明不在早晨播放的原因。
who 修饰 Steven Brown，从句中 trained 与 is 由 but 连接。

sentences.sentence: He says he always has a song in his head and, even more annoying, his mind never seems to make it all the way through.
sentences.translation: 他说自己脑中总有一首歌，更烦人的是，他的大脑似乎从来不能把整首歌完整播放到底。

structure.text: he always has a song in his head
his mind never seems to make it all the way through

difficult_points: says 后省略 that，并列连接他描述的两种情况。
make it all the way through 表示从头到尾完整完成，it 指歌曲的播放。

expressions.text: make it all the way through

sentences.sentence: 'It tends to involve short fragments between, say, 5 or 15 seconds.
sentences.translation: “它往往只包含短短的片段，比如5到15秒。

structure.text: short fragments between, say, 5 or 15 seconds

difficult_points: It 指脑内不完整的歌曲回放。
原文 between... or... 搭配异常，规范表达通常是 between 5 and 15 seconds；say 是插入语。

sentences.sentence: They seem to get looped, for hours sometimes,' he says.
sentences.translation: 这些片段似乎会循环播放，有时一播就是几个小时。”他说。
difficult_points: They 指上一句的 short fragments。
get looped 是 get 被动结构，表示片段进入反复循环的状态。

sentences.sentence: Brown's experience of repeated musical loops may represent a phenomenon called'chunking', in which people remember musical phrases as a single unit of memory, says Caroline Palmer, a psychologist at Ohio State University in Columbus.
sentences.translation: 俄亥俄州立大学心理学家卡罗琳·帕尔默说，布朗经历的音乐片段反复循环，可能代表一种名为“组块化”的现象；在这一过程中，人们会把乐句作为一个完整的记忆单元来记忆。

structure.text: Brown's experience of repeated musical loops
in which people remember musical phrases as a single unit of memory
a psychologist at Ohio State University in Columbus

difficult_points: called'chunking' 修饰 phenomenon，原文 called 与引号之间缺空格。
in which 引导非限制性定语从句，解释 chunking 如何运作。

sentences.sentence: Most listeners have little choice about what chunks they remember.
sentences.translation: 大多数听众几乎无法选择自己会记住哪些组块。
difficult_points: little choice 表示选择余地很少，具有否定含义。
what chunks they remember 是 about 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Particular chunks may be especially 'sticky' if you hear them often or if they follow certain predictable patterns, such as the chord progression of rock 'n' roll music.
sentences.translation: 如果某些组块经常被听到，或遵循某种可预测的模式，例如摇滚乐的和弦进行，它们就可能格外容易黏在脑中。

structure.text: or if they follow certain predictable patterns
such as the chord progression of rock 'n' roll music

difficult_points: 两个 if 从句由 or 连接，分别提出形成黏着记忆的条件。
them 与 they 都指 Particular chunks；such as 举例说明 predictable patterns。

sentences.sentence: Palmer's research shows that the more a piece of music conforms to these patterns, the easier it is to remember.
sentences.translation: 帕尔默的研究表明，一段音乐越符合这些模式，就越容易被记住。

structure.text: that the more a piece of music conforms to these patterns, the easier it is to remember
the more a piece of music conforms to these patterns
the easier it is to remember

difficult_points: that 引导 shows 的宾语从句。
the more..., the easier... 是“越……越……”结构；it 指 a piece of music。

sentences.sentence: That's why you're more likely to be haunted by the tunes of pop music than by those of a classical composer such as J. S. Bach.
sentences.translation: 这就是为什么流行音乐的旋律比巴赫等古典作曲家的旋律更容易在你脑中挥之不去。

structure.text: why you're more likely to be haunted by the tunes of pop music than by those of a classical composer such as J. S. Bach

difficult_points: why 从句作表语，解释前述模式与记忆难易之间的关系。
those 替代 tunes，避免重复；more likely... than... 构成比较。

sentences.sentence: But this ability can be used for good as well as annoyance.
sentences.translation: 但这种能力除了带来烦恼，也可以用于有益的目的。

structure.text: for good as well as annoyance

difficult_points: this ability 指大脑通过模式和组块强化音乐记忆的能力。
as well as 连接 good 与 annoyance，强调它不只有负面作用。

sentences.sentence: Teachers can tap into memory reinforcement by setting their lessons to music.
sentences.translation: 教师可以把课程内容谱成音乐，从而利用记忆强化机制。

structure.text: by setting their lessons to music

difficult_points: tap into 表示开发利用某种资源或机制。
by 后接动名词 setting，说明实现记忆强化的方法。

sentences.sentence: For example, in one experiment students who heard a history text set as the lyrics to a catchy song remembered the words better than those who simply read them, says Sandra Calvert, a psychologist at Georgetown University in Washington DC.
sentences.translation: 华盛顿特区乔治城大学心理学家桑德拉·卡尔弗特说，例如在一项实验中，听到历史课文被改编成朗朗上口歌曲歌词的学生，比只阅读课文的学生记得更牢。

structure.text: who heard a history text set as the lyrics to a catchy song
than those who simply read them
a psychologist at Georgetown University in Washington DC

difficult_points: 第一个 who 修饰 students；heard 后是宾语＋过去分词补语 set as...。
those 替代 students，第二个 who 修饰 those；them 指 the words 或 history text 的内容。

sentences.sentence: This sort of memory enhancement may even explain the origin of music.
sentences.translation: 这种记忆增强作用甚至可能解释音乐的起源。
difficult_points: This sort of memory enhancement 回指把内容配上音乐后更容易记忆的现象。
may 表示关于音乐起源的解释只是一种可能性。

sentences.sentence: Before the written word could be used to record history, people memorised it in songs, says Leon James, a psychologist at the University of Hawaii.
sentences.translation: 夏威夷大学心理学家利昂·詹姆斯说，在文字能够用于记录历史之前，人们把历史编进歌曲加以记忆。

structure.text: Before the written word could be used to record history
a psychologist at the University of Hawaii

difficult_points: Before 从句使用被动语态，说明文字尚不能用于记录历史的时期。
it 指 history；in songs 表示借助歌曲形式记忆。

sentences.sentence: And music may have had an even more important role.'All music has a message.' he says.
sentences.translation: 音乐甚至可能还承担过更重要的角色。“所有音乐都传递着信息。”他说。
difficult_points: may have had 是对过去情况的推测。
原文 role. 与引号之间缺少空格，且引语后的 he 未按常规大写，均原样保留。

sentences.sentence: ‘This message functions to unite society and to standardise the thought processes of people in society.’
sentences.translation: “这种信息的作用是凝聚社会，并使社会成员的思维过程趋于一致。”

structure.text: and to standardise the thought processes of people in society

difficult_points: function to do 表示“发挥作用以……”。
to unite 与 to standardise 是两个并列不定式，说明音乐信息的社会功能。
